commonCitations.statute_of_limitations_tort: 735 ILCS 5/13-202 (2-yr personal injury); 735 ILCS 5/13-212 (medmal: 2-yr discovery / 4-yr repose)
commonCitations.wrongful_death: 740 ILCS 180/1 et seq. (2-yr SOL; 5 yrs for violent intentional conduct)
commonCitations.medical_records_fees_hospital: 735 ILCS 5/8-2001 (handling charge + per-page schedule, IL Comptroller adjusts annually)
commonCitations.hospital_lien: 770 ILCS 23/ Health Care Services Lien Act (aggregate 40% cap; 1/3 per category; if 40% triggered, providers and professionals each capped at 20%)
commonCitations.minor_settlement_approval: 755 ILCS 5/19-8 (Probate Act; GAL appointment + court approval required regardless of amount)
commonCitations.tort_claims_act_notice: 745 ILCS 10/8-101 (Local Governmental Tort Immunity Act — 1-yr SOL against local public entities; 2-yr/4-yr discovery/repose for patient-care claims under (b))
commonCitations.tort_claims_act_deadline_days: 365 days (1 year) under 745 ILCS 10/8-101(a); state-government claims go to Illinois Court of Claims under 705 ILCS 505/
commonCitations.contingency_fee_rule: Ill. R. Prof. C. 1.5(c) (writing required); medmal sliding scale: 33-1/3% of first $150K, 25% of next $850K, 20% over $1M (735 ILCS 5/2-1114)
commonCitations.certificate_of_merit_medmal: 735 ILCS 5/2-622 (attorney affidavit + qualified health-professional report finding 'reasonable and meritorious cause'; 90-day post-filing extension; dismissal under § 2-619 for non-compliance)
commonCitations.damages_cap_medmal_noneconomic: None — 735 ILCS 5/2-1706.5 struck down by Lebron v. Gottlieb Mem'l Hosp., 237 Ill. 2d 217, 930 N.E.2d 895 (2010)
commonCitations.comparative_negligence_standard: 735 ILCS 5/2-1116 (modified comparative; barred only when fault MORE THAN 50%)
commonCitations.threshold_serious_injury: Not applicable — Illinois is at-fault tort state with no PIP threshold; principal allocation gates are 735 ILCS 5/2-1117 (25% several-liability threshold) and 740 ILCS 14/ Dram Shop caps
commonCitations.several_liability_threshold: 735 ILCS 5/2-1117 (defendant <25% at fault: severally liable for non-medical damages; >=25%: joint+several; medical and medically-related damages always joint+several)
commonCitations.attorneys_lien: 770 ILCS 5/1 (Attorneys Lien Act; perfected by written notice)
commonCitations.dram_shop_liability: 235 ILCS 5/6-21 (statutory cause of action; capped damages, annually adjusted)
commonCitations.structured_settlement_protection: 215 ILCS 153/ (court approval for transfer of payment rights)
